titlestmt.titleproper: Guide to the George Eliot and George Henry Lewes Collection
Eliot (George) and George Henry Lewes Collection
titlestmt.author: by Gordon S. Haight and Marjorie G. Wynne

publicationstmt.publisher: Beinecke Rare Book and Manuscript Library

controlnote.p: This finding aid, which is compliant with the Yale EAD Best Practice Guidelines, Version 1.0., has a MARC record in Yale's ILS with the following bib number: 3659107.

maintenanceagency.agencyname: Beinecke Rare Book and Manuscript Library

conventiondeclaration.abbr: beinecke manuscript unit archival processing manual

descriptivenote.p: Finding aid description and metadata in Archives at Yale are licensed under a CC0 1.0 Universal (CC0 1.0) Public Domain Dedication. This means that anyone may copy, modify, and distribute this descriptive metadata without restriction or permission. The CC0 license is explained in full on the Creative Commons website: CC0 1.0 Universal (CC0 1.0) Public Domain Dedication. More information is available on our Help page.

maintenanceevent.eventdescription: This finding aid was produced using ArchivesSpace on Saturday November 7, 2020 at 06:38
Transformed with yale.addEadidUrl.xsl. Adds @url with handle for finding aid. Overwrites @url if already present.
beinecke.eliot.xml converted for compliance with Yale EAD Best Practice Guidelines with brbl-migrate-01.xsl (mr2007-08-13).

PUBLIC "-//Yale University::Beinecke Rare Book and Manuscript Library//TEXT (US::CtYBR::::[GEORGE ELIOT AND GEORGE HENRY LEWES COLLECTION ])//EN" "eliot.xml" converted from EAD 1.0 to 2002 by v1to02.xsl (sy2003-10-15).

did.unittitle: George Eliot and George Henry Lewes collection

corpname.part: Beinecke Rare Book and Manuscript Library

did.abstract: The collection includes correspondence by, to and about Eliot and Lewes. There are 4 journals, 1 diary, several notebooks with notes for a projected novel, quotes, poetry, and early school essays all belonging to George Eliot, accompanied by reviews and scripts of dramatizations of Daniel Deronda in the 1920s and 1970s. With 3 journals, 9 diaries, and notebooks with research notes belonging to George Henry Lewes, accompanied by miscellaneous writings, including several chapters of Problems of Life and Mind. There is also printed material, reviews, memorabilia, and photograph portraits of Eliot and Lewes as well as artwork by others. Principal correspondents include members of the Lewes family, Caroline and Charles Bray, John Emerich Edward Dalberg Acton, Barbara Leigh Smith Bodichon, John Chapman, Anna Chalmers and J. W. Cross, Alexandre D'Albert-Durade, Frederic Harrison, Sara Sophia Hennell, Frances Lucy Evans Houghton, Maria Lewis, Edward Robert Bulwer Lytton, Herbert Spencer, and Anthony Trollope.

accessrestrict.head: Conditions Governing Access
accessrestrict.p: The materials are open for research.

acqinfo.head: Immediate Source of Acquisition
acqinfo.p: Acquired by gift and purchase from various sources.

prefercite.head: Preferred Citation
prefercite.p: George Eliot and George Henry Lewes Collection. General Collection, Beinecke Rare Book and Manuscript Library.

processinfo.head: Processing Information
processinfo.p: The collection is comprised of material formerly classed as: MS Vault Eliot.

This finding aid was created in the early 1980s as a typewritten list, and was converted into a ASCII data file by means of scanning and Optical Character Recognition software. While attempts have been made to retain the complete information from the original document, a number of format changes have been made to present the structure of this archive in accordance with current practice.
Microfilms of manuscripts owned by other libraries or individuals formerly held in this collection (as Section XI. Films of Manuscripts) were discarded by curatorial request April 1997.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: After George Eliot's death there were four principal groups of her manuscripts and private papers held by the families of Evans, Blackwood, Lewes, and Cross. Her family letters were at Derby in the hands of her grandnieces, the Evans sisters. Her long correspondence about the publication of her books was in the Edinburgh office of William Blackwood and Sons, who have since deposited it in the National Library of Scotland. The Blackwoods also owned the manuscript of Scenes of Clerical Life, which was sold in 1911 to the Pierpont Morgan Library. The manuscripts of her other works George Eliot bequeathed with all her literary rights to Charles Lee Lewes, her residuary legatee and sole executor of her estate. In 1891 his widow presented these manuscripts (except for "Brother Jacob" and "A College Breakfast-Party"), bound in 24 volumes, to the British Museum. On June 27, 1923 her three daughters sold many of the remaining George Eliot papers at Sotheby's; they were widely dispersed.

Charles Lewes had lent George Eliot's letters and journals to John Walter Cross, who was writing George Eliot's Life (1885). Apparently believing that they belonged to him, Cross retained them. On his death in 1924 they passed to his niece Miss Elsie Druce, who during the 1930's disposed of most of them in several sales at Sotheby's. Through the zealous interest of Professor Chauncey Brewster Tinker, who had been appointed Keeper of Rare Books in 1930, many of them were purchased for Yale. In that year he secured the largest single group, consisting of 620 letters to the Brays and the Hennells, which with 46 early letters to Maria Lewis formed the nucleus of the Yale collection. In 1931 George Eliot's Commonplace Book and her most important Journal (1854-61) were also acquired, as well as her Diary for 1880, and the travel journals of her trips to Germany (1858), to Italy (1860 and 1864), and to Normandy (1865). From his own collection Mr. Tinker gave among other manuscripts George Eliot's Journal (1861-77). Yale thus has all the extant journals and diaries except the diary for 1879, which is in the Berg Collection at the New York Public Library.

From Lewes's last surviving granddaughter Mrs. E. Carrington Ouvry (1877-1974), Yale secured the manuscript of "Brother Jacob" bound with that of "A College Breakfast-Party", and George Eliot's translation of Spinoza's Ethics. Of the greatest value to scholars are all of Lewes's extant journals and diaries except that for 1878, of which there is a microfilm. The manuscripts of five of Lewes's plays are included in the collection. There are several notebooks used for his Biographical History of Philosophy and Goethe's Life, and for his work on physiology and psychology, as well as many fragments of his Problems of Life and Mind, of which George Eliot completed and published the final volume.

Autograph letters have been added continually since the collection began. In 1975 there were more than 1030 by George Eliot and 220 by G. H. Lewes. Of letters to and about them there were more than 1040. The earliest family letters, including the only known one to George Eliot's father, were purchased in 1969 from the widow of his great-great-grandson Robert Evans, and George Eliot's letters to John Walter Cross and his family were purchased from Miss Druce's heir, Mr. Ian Maclean, in 1970.

userestrict.head: Conditions Governing Use
userestrict.p: The George Eliot and George Henry Lewes Collection is the physical property of the Beinecke Rare Book and Manuscript Library, Yale University. Literary rights, including copyright, belong to the authors or their legal heirs and assigns. For further information, consult the appropriate curator.

Letter, You have not, I dare say...
Letter, Monday I find it is in vain...
Letter, Saturday Father is not apparently...
Letter, Saturday evening Father is going on well…
Letter, Sunday evening There is no material alteration...
Letter, Monday evening Father has had a more...
Letter, Typescript only, I have been holding...
Letter, Thursday evening Father is very much altered...
Letter, Wednesday evening I know you are anxious to hear...
Letter, Wednesday How have the weeks passed...

Letter, to GE, I have read your new book...
Letter, to GE, Friday. I have had some qualms...

Letter, to J.W. Cross, In the important passage...
Letter, to J.W. Cross, I am delighted with the success...
Letter, I have returned the paper...
Letter, A ship you see last week...
Letter, With a copy of his letter to GHL
Letter, to Elizabeth Rebecca (Brabant) Hennell Call

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Draft. Accompanied by Frederick Temple's judgment in the case of Craddock vs. Bray, 12 July 1860.

did.unittitle: Bray-Hennell Extracts, all in the hand of Sara Sophia Hennell:
1. Extracts from the letters of Sara Hennell, Mary Hennell, and Caroline Hennell Bray concerning GE.
2. Extracts of "quotable passages" from the letters of GE to Maria Lewis, John Sibree, and Mary Sibree Cash.
3. Account of an incident in the childhood of GE, told by Miss Shaw.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: On verso: List of GHL's manuscripts sold to Yale

scopecontent.p: In a wrapper with genealogy of Elinor Southwood Lewes Ouvry on front cover. [to Edward and George Henry Lewes] A true, literal & exact account of the Goods, Chattels & other appurtenances of Edgar James Lewes. 10 quatrains of comic verse, signed. 2 p.

Portsmouth, Eveline Alicia Juliana (Herbert), Countess of, 1834-1906
Letter, You are no new friend...
Letter, I think it very good of you...
Accepting Burton's portrait of GE for National Portrait Gallery.
Engraved form accepting "three specimens of the original hand-writing" of GE.
Senior, Jane Elizabeth (Hughes), (Mrs. Nassau John Senior), 1828-1877
Letter, with quotation from G.E. Maurice
Letter, to J. W. Parker & Son, Asking about GHL's Biographical History of Philosophy
Letter, Many thanks for your criticisims...
Siebold, Karl Theodor Ernst von, 1804-1885, and Antonie von Siebold
Letter, to GE, Enclosing a poem entitled "New Lives"
Memorandum of agreement concerning the publication of GHL's Studies of Animal Life
Smith, Lucy Caroline (Cumming), (Mrs. William Henry Smith)

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: with 12 pages of extracts on aphasia, etc.

did.unittitle: Stuart, Evorilda Eliza Maria (Fraser) (Mrs. John Glass Gordon Stuart), 1837?-1903
Taylor, Clementia (Doughty), d. 1908 (Mrs. Peter Alfred Taylor)

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Judgment in the case of Craddock vs. Bray (12 July 1860) filed with letter from Charles Bray to C.H. Bracebridge, 14 July 1860.

did.unittitle: Tollemache, Marguerite Home (Purvis), (Mrs. William Augustus Tollemache), 1817-1896
Letter, addressed also to GHL and filed with Trollope's letters to him
Fragment, saying: I shall be at Greenwich on the 9th
Letter, Accompanied by a printed map of Trollope's Barsetshire.
Trollope, Frances Eleanor (Ternan), (Mrs. Thomas Adolphus Trollope)
Agreement for publication of Problems of Life & Mind , addressed to GHL.
Statement on royalties due for Problems of Life & Mind , addressed to C.L. Lewes.

relatedmaterial.head: Related Materials
relatedmaterial.p: See: Waldstein, Charles

did.unittitle: Letter, Accompanied by a copy of selected passages from a letter from GE to E.S. Phelps, sent to GE to be copied by her after the original was accidentally destroyed.
List of articles on the age of the earth prepared for GE at the request of Warren De la Rue.
Willim, Elizabeth (Ashweek) Lewes, (Mrs. John G. Willim, GHL's mother), 1787-1870
Letter, to GE, with draft of reply by C.L. Lewes on verso
Letter, to GE, On behalf of the Physiological Society

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Contains recollections of Weimar (1854), Berlin (1854-55), Ilfracombe (1856), the Scilly Isles (1857), Jersey (1857); "How I came to write Fiction"; and the "History of Adam Bede." 152 leaves 21 cm. Accompanied by typescript prepared by Richard L. Purdy.

acqinfo.head: Immediate Source of Acquisition
acqinfo.p: Sold by Miss Elsie Druce, niece of J. W. Cross, at Sotheby's on 17 April 1930, item 911. Purchased with the Altschul Fund, 1931.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Contains journal of a trip to Germany, April 14-October 27, 1858 and beginning at the back of the book, "Recollections of Italy, 1860." 175 p. Accompanied by typescript prepared by Richard L. Purdy.

acqinfo.head: Immediate Source of Acquisition
acqinfo.p: Sold by Miss Elsie Druce, niece of J. W. Cross, at Sotheby's on 17 April 1930, item 915. Purchased with the Altschul Fund.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: "Order of writings", a list of GE's works up to 1876, precedes the first page of the diary. 144 p. [53 blank] 15 cm. Accompanied by typescript prepared by Richard L. Purdy.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Contains journal of a trip to Italy in 1864 and to Normandy in 1865. 137 p. [74 blank] 12 cm. Accompanied by typescript prepared by Richard L. Purdy.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Contains also cash accounts for the year 1880. Accompanied by typescript prepared by Richard L. Purdy.

acqinfo.head: Immediate Source of Acquisition
acqinfo.p: Purchased from Gabriel Wells with the Library Associates Fund, 1935.

acqinfo.head: Immediate Source of Acquisition
acqinfo.p: Sold by Miss Elsie Druce, niece of J.W. Cross, at Sotheby's on 17 April 1930, item 913. Purchased with the Altschul Fund, 1931.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Contains poetry and prose including In a London Drawing room; Ex Oriente Lux; Arms! To Arms!; In the South; Erinna; The Death of Moses; Mottoes (Felix Holt); Notes on Form in Art; Versification. The dates at the end of the poems (1865-1869) indicate probable dates of composition. 73 leaves [31 blank and 3 cut out].

acqinfo.head: Immediate Source of Acquisition
acqinfo.p: Purchased from Gabriel Wells with the A. H. Childs Fund. 1950.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Contains miscellaneous notes and quotations, some in Greek and German. 20 p. 11 cm.

acqinfo.head: Immediate Source of Acquisition
acqinfo.p: Sold by Miss Elsie Druce, niece of J.W. Cross, at Sotheby's on 9 April 1935, item 435. Gift of Chauncey B. Tinker, 1955. Tinker 867

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Notes, possibly for a projected novel, on the back of a card calendar for January 1876. 1 card. 9.5 x 13.5 cm.

acqinfo.head: Immediate Source of Acquisition
acqinfo.p: Purchased from C.A. Stonehill with the Library Associates Fund, 1944. Published in Blanche Colton Williams' George Eliot, a biography , New York, Macmillan, 1936. p. 290-291.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Notebook of memoranda, statistics, historical notes and quotations collected in preparation for writing Felix Holt , The Spanish Gypsy . 150 p. 14 cm.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: GE's translation of Spinoza's Ethica , parts 1-5. 654 leaves 23 cm. Accompanied by GHL's "Notes and additions to the Theologico-Political Tractate " of Spinoza. 9 leaves.

acqinfo.head: Immediate Source of Acquisition
acqinfo.p: Purchased from Mrs. Elinor Ouvry with the Library Associates Fund, 1942.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Notebook with addresses in the hands of GE and GHL. 64 leaves 14.5 cm.

acqinfo.head: Immediate Source of Acquisition
acqinfo.p: Purchased from C. A. Stonehill with the Library Associates Fund, 1944.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: This notebook, the earliest known GE manuscript, contains poems copied from various sources, rules and illustrative problems in arithmetic, an essay on "Affectation and Conceit," and a story, "Edward Neville." 66 leaves.

acqinfo.head: Immediate Source of Acquisition
acqinfo.p: Purchased from J. H. P. Pafford with the Library Associates Fund.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: A short story published in the Cornhill Magazine , July 1864, and a poem, "A College Breakfast Party," April 1874, published in Macmillan's Magazine , July 1878. 128 leaves, probably bound by C.L. Lewes, 1881.

acqinfo.head: Immediate Source of Acquisition
acqinfo.p: Purchased from Mrs. Ouvry with the Library Associates Fund.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Of the agreement with Smith, Elder & Co. relating to the copyright of Romola . With note on verso by Mrs. Ouvry.

acqinfo.head: Immediate Source of Acquisition
acqinfo.p: Purchased from Mrs. Ouvry with the Library Associates Fund.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: 4 p. removed from one of the notebooks for The Spanish Gypsy , 1868. Removed from v. 25 of GE's Works , Boston, 1908.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: A poem. Two versions. First version dated March 1865. 1 p. 23 cm. Second version dated September 1866 but on paper watermarked 1869 and in the violet ink first used in 1872. 1 p. 23 cm.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Contains a few brief chronological notes on ancient history. Paper cover marked "Quarry". 26 p. 9 cm.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Criticism of Joseph Remi Léopold Delboeuf's article, "George-Henry Lewes. Sa vie et ses ouvrages" which appeared in La Revue Scientifique for 1 February 1879. 1 p. 15 cm.

acqinfo.head: Immediate Source of Acquisition
acqinfo.p: Sold at Sotheby's on 27 October 1959; gift of Gordon S. Haight and Richard L. Purdy in honor of Chauncey B. Tinker, 1959.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: The original diary is in the Berg Collection of the New York Public Library, and permission to paraphrase or quote from this diary must be obtained from the Berg Collection. This typescript was prepared by R. H. Nims for Gordon S. Haight.

originalsloc.head: Existence and Location of Originals
originalsloc.p: Photocopy only, original at Nuneaton. This photocopy was made from a photocopy owned by Mrs. Joy Hooton, December 1984.

did.unittitle: Section V. George Eliot. WRITINGS. DRAMATIZATIONS.
1. Program and review of Daniel Deronda dramatized by Lydia Lewisohn and Lily Tobias, "Q" Theatre, Kew, 14-20 February 1927. 2 pieces.
2. Daniel Deronda , dramatized in 6 episodes for BBC by Alexander Baron, 1969. Recorded 14 June-4 August 1969; transmitted 11 April-13 May 1970. 365 leaves.
3. Review of the first episode of Alexander Baron's dramatization of Daniel Deronda . Manuscript note on clipping: Telegraph 3-16-70.
Section VI. George Henry Lewes. WRITINGS

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Miscellaneous notes, quotations, sketches. 144 p. Written mostly in pencil.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Miscellaneous notes, addresses, diary entries in Antwerp and Holland. 148 p. Written mostly in pencil.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Miscellaneous notes, addresses, quotations about Shakespeare, Pythagoras, etc. Several entries in ink. 110 p.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: 127 p. (46 blank). "The Oedipus Colonos of Sophocles. Translated by G.H.L. 1843 (Sept -)" 16 p. at end.

did.unittitle: 16. "Ariadne: or The Principles of Success and Failure in Literature."

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Notebook, 66 leaves (4 blank). Published as "The Principles of Success and Failure in Literature" in the Fortnightly Review vol. 1-2, 1865.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: 58 leaves (48 blank). Probably miscellaneous notes for his Problems of Life and Mind .

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: 138 leaves (86 blank). Probably miscellaneous notes for his Problems in Life and Mind .

did.unittitle: 20. "Historical Note Books IV, V, VI centuries."
21. "[Historical Note Books] VII & VIII cents. IX & X cents."

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: some apparently for a Volume IV never published to be entitled 'The Study of Psychology"' Notes by GHL, GE, and others.
Published as the third series of Problems of Life and Mind , 1879.
Note: Larger portions of this manuscript are at Princeton, Huntington, and Berg.

did.unittitle: a. A list of 13 items beginning: George Eliot's copied notes from various notebooks of G. H. Lewes' philosophy." Miscellaneous notes on verso, one headed "Belief." l leaf.
b. Table of contents in the hands of GHL and GE of the former's "The Study of Psychology, its object, scope, & method." 1 p.
d. "V. Laws of Matter in Mind." 24 leaves.
e. Miscellaneous notes beginning with "Ruskin." 4 leaves.
f. "Book II, Chap. I. The Alexandrian School." 24 leaves.
g. "Notes on fragment of Parmenides printed in Lewes' Hist. Mat. pp. 54, 55." 3 leaves.
h. "Remarks on Mr. Lewes' Chapters IV...VII." 16 p.
i. "Being & existence are here treated as identical terms..." 24 p. numbered E to FF. Pencilled note on E: "Wm. Appleton?"
25. "The Fox who got the Grapes. A comedy in One Act." 42 leaves.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Note on cover: "Turned into 'Mrs. Beauchamp's Vengeance' in Blackwood."

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: "Thoughts of G.H. Lewes", cover title in Mrs. Ouvry's hand.

did.unittitle: 30. "Pretension; or The School for Parvenus. A Comedy in 5 Acts."

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Possibly the first draft of the chapter on this subject in the second series of The Problems of Life and Mind . 21 leaves numbered 166-[177].

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Part of Vol. III, Part I, etc. in folder labelled "Colour, Sensation." 103 p.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Part of vol. IV, in George Eliot's hand. 6 leaves.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Chapters XI-XIV of Problem III: "The Sphere of Sense and Logic of Feeling." (Third series). 69 leaves.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Notes on "Insanity" (in folder marked "Hallucinations") probably intended for another volume of Problems of Life and Mind . 24 leaves.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Appendix B of vol. II: "Lagrange and Hegel: The Speculative Method." 28 leaves numbered 257-284.

did.unittitle: 40. Notes of requirements for examination in classical languages, history, geography, logic, and political philosophy. 1 p.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: GHL's sketch of a tragedy in 3 acts adapted from Carl Dietrich Ludwig Felix von Rumohr, "Der letzte Savello," published in Urania. Taschenbuch auf das Jahr 1834 , Leipzig, 1834 and re- printed in Deutscher Novellenschatz , ed. P. Heyse and H. Kurz, Munich, II (1871), 125-209. The Savello-Cassandra episode is found in pp. 184-209. Written in pencil by GHL and traced over by GE in ink. GHL mentions reading the story in his Diary, 2 August 1871.

did.unittitle: "Notes and additions to the Theologico- Political Tractate" of Spinoza: notes accompanying GE's translation of Spinoza's Ethics .
Section VII. George Eliot. ASSOCIATION ITEMS

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: The first book GE received from her father. Inscribed on flyleaf [1870?] and presented to J. W. Cross in 1880.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Agatha , London, 1869, and How Lisa Loved the King , Boston, 1869, bound together with an inscription to Mrs. William Cross, January, 1874. Additions and corrections in GE's hand on pages 10, 16, 18, 24, 34, 37, 47, and pencil markings for reading aloud.

did.unittitle: 3. Thomas à Kempis' De Imitatione Christi , 1853.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Presented by GE to John Walter Cross on their wedding day. Inscribed by Cross "JWC from MAC 6 May 1880".

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: A notebook with M. E. L. stamped on cover contains one note (the address of Josiah Mills, coal merchant, now almost obliterated) in GE's hand and several by J. W. Cross, soon after her death, chiefly dates drawn probably from her journals.

did.unittitle: Section VIII. George Eliot. WRITINGS ABOUT.
1. List of GE's manuscripts made by Charles Lee Lewes, probably in 1880.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: With a note by Mrs. Ouvry: "Most of these sold 1923 at Sotheby." 3 p. 18 cm.

did.unittitle: Emile Montégut, "Le roman réaliste en Angleterre" in Revue des deux mondes, II 21, p. 867-897 [p. 867- 894 missing).
c. THE MILL ON THE FLOSS
Wilhelm Scherer in Deutsche Rundschau, 10, p. 240-255.
R. E. Francillon in Gentleman's Magazine, 17, p. 410-427.
J. Jacobs in Macmillan's Magazine, 36, p. 101-111.
E. P. Whipple in North American Review, 124, p. 31-52.
Unidentified, headed "Literature of the Day," p. 645-648.
Miss C. H. Spence, Melbourne Review p. [146]-163.
Estimates of the cost of publishing GE's works

relatedmaterial.head: Related Archival Materials note
relatedmaterial.p: See: correspondence with Wm. Blackwood & Sons.

did.unittitle: Adam Bede, Mill on the Floss, Silas Marner, Felix Holt.
Statement of sales of GE's works 23 pieces.
a. Register of Investments: autograph list by John Walter Cross with a few entries by GE and later entries by Cross. 90 p. 21 cm.
b. "Schedule of Stock & Share Certificates belonging to Mrs. Lewes," with marginal notes by GE. 1 p. 33 cm.
c. "Memorandum of Property deposited with The Union Bank of London Charing Cross Branch in the name of Mrs. M. E. Lewes." 1 p. 34 cm.
d. "To the Holders of India £4 per- Cent Stock...": printed notice, endorsed by C. L. Lewes: "Mrs. Müllers Trust" [probably Mrs. Eduard Müller, wife of the headmaster of Hofwyl School].
6. Certified copy of the record of GE's baptism, 29 April 1819. Signed by G. R. Pennington, Chilvers Coton, 30 April 1881. 1 p.
7. Deed of declaration adopting the surname of Lewes in addition to the name of Evans. Signed Mary Ann Evans and witnessed by Charles Lee Lewes, with envelope addressed by GE. 2 p. 33 cm.
8. Photocopies of extracts from the settlement by GE on her marriage to J. W. Cross and from her will, pre- pared by Duncan, Warren and Gardner, solicitors, 8 p. 32 cm.
9. Photocopy of the announcement in The Times of GE's marriage to J. W. Cross. 1 card. 10.5 cm.
Receipt for GE's burial charges, paid by J. W. Cross
Account of GE's funeral, from The Times .
11. Agreement between Mrs. Elinor Ouvry and Mary Margaret Paterson about George Eliot's Family Life and Letters by Arthur Henry Paterson. 2 p.
a. The Author's Syndicate, signed by Margaret Sanders.
13. Reviews of Arthur Henry Paterson's George Eliot's Family Life and Letters mounted in red notebook.
Clippings of correspondence about GE and the Lewes family by:
Manuscript letters relating to the correspondence in The Spectator :
Adamson, Mary M., ALS to Mrs. Ouvry.
Ouvry, Elinor. Draft of a letter to Mary M. Adamson.
Adamson, Mary M.. ALS to Mrs. Ouvry.
Buxton, Violet., ALS to Mrs. Ouvry.
15. Paris, Bernard J. "George Eliot's Unpublished Poetry"

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: [a study of Eliot IV.7] reprinted from Studies in Philology , LVI, 3, July 1959. 3 copies.

did.unittitle: 16. Taylor, Don. "The Confessions of Marian Evans. A Portrait of George Eliot."

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Typescript of the dramatization pre- sented on BBC television. 63 p.

did.unittitle: 17. Currey, Stella Martin. "Scenes from an Improper Life."

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Program for the dramatization pre- sented at Leamington Theatre, April 19-22, 24-29, 1972.

did.unittitle: 18. Questions about the original manuscript of Middlemarch.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Offering the corrected proofs of the works of George Eliot from the Archives of the Publishers William Blackwood and Sons, Ltd. 1857-1880." Catalogue of Lew David Feldman, House of El Dieff, Inc.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: George Eliot. A Biography. Author's corrected typescript and proof sheets of the biography published by the Oxford University Press in 1968.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: "Sof'ya Vasil'yevna Kovalevskaya". Offprint from Mathematical Chronicle , v.5, 1976-77.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Typescript of his notes of a conver- sation with George Eliot.

did.unittitle: Section IX. George Eliot. SPECIAL FILES

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: The Priory, 21 North Bank, Regents Park. 3 sheets, folded. The Heights, Witley, nr. Godalming. 1 half-sheet. 1 plain envelope.

did.unittitle: 2. Calling card of Mr. & Mrs. Cross
3. Photograph of GE's blotter, with extracts from various authors, notes for poems, etc., mounted in a folder.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: 26 cm. Three smaller pieces of genuine GE blotting paper laid in. 19 cm.

did.unittitle: 4. Photographs of GE or said to be of GE
a. Originals in the National Portrait Gallery:
No. 669 Chalk drawing by F. W. Burton. 5 copies.
No. 1232 Watercolor by Mrs. C. Bray. 2 copies.
No. 1405 Copy of a portrait by F. D'Albert Durade, the original of which is in the Gulson Library, Coventry.
No. 1758 Pencil sketch by Lady Alma-Tadema.
No. 2211 Pencil sketch by Lady Alma-Tadema.
No. 2645 Silhouette, artist unknown. Not GE
b. Glass negative of D'Albert Durade's portrait of GE, made by Howard A. Kelly.
c. Photograph of a painting by Sophus Williams.
d. Photograph of GE made by the London Stereoscopic and Photographic Company, 2 copies.
e. Photograph of pencil sketch by GE made by Princess Louise. The original in the possession of Mrs. Reginald Allen, New York City.
f. Photograph of a chalk drawing of GE by Samuel Laurence.
g. J. W. Cross's framed copy of Rajon's etching from the 1858 photograph.
i. Full face, with hood. Not GE.
j. Two pencil sketches of GE by Barbara Bodichon pasted on flyleaves of Romola .
a. Doorway of Griff House, Chilvers Coton.
6. Poems written to or sent to GE
1. O painter, match an English bloom…
2. Why should I strive to express it?...
4. O when through all the crowd...
b. Her eyes were like two quiet stars...
c. T. George Eliot, signed A. E. I.
e. To the Author of George Eliot's Gospel.
f. To the Writer of "How we reward research"!
g. [Poem in German] "Noch gläntze im warmenden Sonnenlicht..."
h. [Poem in German] "Welch' tiefer Ton dringt an das Ohr?"
i. "Paraphrase on a part of the conclusion of The Mill on the Floss".
j. Sigrutz, Karl. "The Spanish Gipsey by George Elliot".
k. Stanzas from J. R. Lowell's The Present Crisis .

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Copy made by Mrs. Hans William Sotheby and sent to GE.

did.unittitle: 7. Analysis of the water at Witley
8. Quotation from Middlemarch used as subject for an essay on the "Examination for First Appointments to the Cavalry and Infantry".
9. signed by A.V.A. Water color illustration labelled "Daniel Deronda may he come in?"
10. Braddon, Paul. Eight water color sketches of places connected with GE in folder labelled "Reminiscences of George Eliot". 8 X 10 inches.
a. Announcements, programs, etc. for The George Eliot Centennial Conference at the University of Puget Sound, 4 pieces.
b. George Eliot. An exhibition at the British Library.
c. Daniel Waley. George Eliot's Blotter. 2 copies.
d. Commemorative stamps of George Eliot, Charlotte Brontë, Emily Brontë and Mrs. Gaskell, with an enlarged photograph of the GE stamp. 5 pieces.
e. Westminster Abbey. Unveiling and dedication of a memorial to George Eliot. With ticket of admission. 2 pieces.
f. Gordon Haight's speech in Westminster Abbey on 21 June, when a memorial stone to George Eliot was unveiled. Photocopy of the speech printed in the London Review of Books, 17 July - 6 August 1980.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: transcript of an anonymous journal in the Nuneaton Public Library. 1810-45 (1838-June 1843 lacking). 168 pages. Accompanied by a manuscript list ("from Mrs. Dodds 18 Sept 1950") of the most striking events in the journal.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Miscellaneous announcements, photographs, clippings, reports, etc.

did.unittitle: The George Eliot Fellowship Review, No. 1, June 1970-

otherfindaid.head: Other Finding Aids
otherfindaid.p: See: 1984 +S18. This item has been catalogued separately.

did.unittitle: Section X. George Henry Lewes. SPECIAL FILES
1. Articles and stories: printed and proofs

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Proof of an article printed by the Contemporary Review but never published, nor was it used in the section on attention in Problems of Life and Mind , 3rd series, 1879, p. 184-196.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Four parts signed Vivian [GHL] and published in Douglas Jerrold's Shilling Magazine (May-August 1847) 436-448, 509-519, 35-45, 168-176.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Second revise proof, printed by Wm. Clowes & Son. Problems of Life and Mind , 1879, 3rd series, Problem III, ch. 7, p. 312-325, uses parts of it, mostly rewritten.

did.unittitle: h. "A night in a German swamp."
i. "On the dread and dislike of science."
k. "Trust not your eyes in love" (followed by) "Men of thought and men of action."

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Notes for lectures on Hamlet . Passages cut from printed text with manuscript notes interpolated. 46 [i.e. 40] p. in paper cover marked "Hamlet."

did.unittitle: 3. Syllabus of a course of six lectures on the history of philosophical specu- lation, ancient and modern. Printed syllabus of lectures given at the Liverpool Mechanics Institution in February 1849.
4. Estimates and statements of sales of his books.
5. Reviews of The Life and Works of Goethe
c. Schöll, Adolf. Weimarer Sontags - Blat. 13 and 27
d. Siegfried, Heinrich. An G.H. Lewes eine Epistel . Berlin.
6. Reviews of Problems of Life and Mind :
a. "Mr. Lewes and Metaphysics" in unidentified periodical.
b. Bartling, Heinrich. Allgemeine Literarische Correspondenz, p. 1-2.
f. Sully, James. New Quarterly Magazine , p. 356-376.
g. Delboeuf, M. J. Revue Scientifique , p. 730-733.
8. George Henry Lewes Studentship, University of Cambridge, founded by George Eliot in 1879.
a. Letters, etc., relating to the founding of the studentship:
1. Balfour, Francis Maitland, 1851-1882. ALS to Sir Michael Forster, 7 p.
2. Dyer, William T. Thistleton. Memorandum, 11 p.
3. Eliot, George. Draft of a plan for the studentship. 3 p.
4. Forster, Sir Michael, 1836- 1907. 3 ALS to GE, with 2 drafts of plans. May 21, 26, and Jun 10. 2, 4, 4, 6, 9 p.
5. Huxley, Thomas Henry Comments on a proposal for the studentship. 2 p.
6. Paget, Sir James, 1814-1899. ALS to GE, 3 p.
8. Sidgwick, Henry, 5 ALS to GE. March 2, 4, 12, May 8, 16. 8, 2, 4, 3, 4 p.
9. Sidgwick, Henry. ALS to Sir Michael Forster.
10. Warren, Charles Harris, 2 ALS to GE. 4, 3 p.
11. Printed "Extract from the Conditions under which is constituted a Studentship of Physiology founded in Memory of George Henry Lewes." 2 p.
12. Printed "Trust Deed of the "'George Henry Lewes' Studentship." 4 p. 2 copies.
13. Roy, Charles Smart. (the first GHL Student) 2 ALS to GE 12, 4 p.
b. Letters from applicants for the Studentship:

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: 1. A., the Rev. M. 2. Allen, Frank J. 3. Anderson, L. 4. Bailey, T. R. 5. Bellhouse, F. W. 6. Cameron, W. J. 7. Combs, Annie L. 8. Cooper, Henry Charles Evans 9. Draper, John B., with copies of 3 testimonials

10. Emmett, R. B. 11. Field, John 12. Harris, D. P. 13. Heath, Alice 14. Huson - More, J. 15. Kempe, C. P. 16. Kent, W. Saville, with printed testimonials 17. Logan, J. R. 18. Mead, George B. 19. Newman, James H.
20. North, W. 21. Osborn, Ada E. 22. Payne, Georgianna 23. Plummer, H. G. 24. Radford, William 25. Smith, W. F. 26. Waters, W. H. 27. Wheatley, Arthur 28. Younge, George Harrison

did.unittitle: c. Correspondence between Ernest Carrington Ouvry and the University of Cambridge relating to the GHL Studentship.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: 11 items, including one leaf of notes by Mrs. Ouvry, a list of some of the early Students, and a printed announcement of the vacant Studentship on 1 October 1946.

did.unittitle: d. Photographs and drawings of GHL.
1. Sketch by Anna Gliddon. Photocopy of the original in the National Portrait Gallery. No. 1373.
2. Sketch of Agnes Jervis Lewes, George Henry Lewes, and Thornton Leigh Hunt, about 1844. Photocopy of a pencil sketch by W. M. Thackeray in the National Portrait Gallery.
3. Sketch by Rudolf Lehman, dated. Photocopy of the original in the Nuneaton Public Library.
a. Noble, D. George Henry Lewes, George Eliot and the Physiological Society. Removed from The Journal of Physiology, December 1976, vol. 263, no. 1, p. 45P-54P.
